post.content:
Recently I interviewed at UBER and it was a Datastructures and algorihtms round.

The question was as follow:

Givent a infinite stream of events and events are like:

user1 city1 BOOKED       timestamp1
user2 city1 CANCELLED timestamp2
user3 city1 BOOKED       timestamp3
user4 city1 BOOKED       timestamp4
user1 city2 ENDED          timestamp5



On these events we have to build a system where query can be anything like

1. How many cabs are BOOKED between timestamp1 to timestamp4.
2. How many cabse are BOOKED in city1 and ended in city2 between timestamp1 to timestamp4.


Expectation of this round was to build some generic datastructure that can support query of anytype.

Expectation1- How we are going to ingest these events, since its a DS/ALGO round so a in memory datastructure was expected.
Expectation2- Support any types of query.

Can anyone help me in understanding what is the expected solution for this problem?

**NOTES: Event may not be in sorted order by timestamp. a event with timestamp ts1 can come at time ts3.**

HashMap<City, TreeMap<TimeStamp, BookingID>>: City Is the Key -> Value is TreeMap -> TreeMap Key -> Booking time in nano seconds, Value is Booking ID
Map<ID, BookingID> map - store the booking ID, Booking details: BookingMap
Map<String, List<BookingID>> : User Booking Map: Stores User Booking

Booking Details:
ID - Some unique number: Use Atomic Integer - initialize it with 1
class Booking {
	int id,
	String user;
	long time;
        String Startcity:
        String endcity
        String status; (Make it an enum)
}

1. When User Make a booking: public int MakeBooking(User Id, startCity, endCity, time) {
Step:
	1. Create Booking Object -> increment the atomic integer to get the booking ID
	2. Create an entry into BookingMap
	2. insert into UserBookingMap
	3. insert into City Booking Map.
}
Question:
1. How many cabs are BOOKED between timestamp1 to timestamp4.
Solution: Go to CityBookingMap -> for each entry in the city map -> get all bookings between the time stamps.(Sorted timeStamp
2. How many cabs are BOOKED in city1 and ended in city2 between timestamp1 to timestamp4.
Solution: Go to CityBookingMap -> for each entry in the city map -> get all bookings between the time stamps and aff filter based on status and endCity

bookingMap: TreeMap<TimeStamp, Pair<User, City>> : contains BOOKED data
endedMap: TreeMap<TimeStamp, Pair<User, City>> :: contains ENDED data

How many cabs are BOOKED between timestamp1 to timestamp4.
-> bookingMap.subMap(timestamp1, timestamp4).size();
How many cabse are BOOKED in city1 and ended in city2 between timestamp1 to timestamp4.
-> bookingMap.subMap(timestamp1, timestamp4).values().stream().filter(v -> v.getSecond() == city1).map(v -> v.getFirst())
-> same for endedMap
-> Sets.intersection()
